methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $39M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 9 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Liminal Insights is a Series A-stage climate and energy company founded by Barry Van Tassell and Shaurjo Biswas that has raised $38.9M to date. The broader Climate & Energy sector encompasses 9 peer companies tracked across public and private markets, including 1 public comparable (LNZA) and 8 private peers such as Helion, Redwood Materials, QuantumScape, KoBold Metals, and Electric Hydrogen. However, the peer set lacks sufficient density at the same or adjacent stage with comparable valuation tier to support a robust peer-median multiple approach.

Given data limitations, a stage-progression method was applied. Using the $39M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, this method implies an enterprise value of $78M. This represents the single triangulation point available from the dataset.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $66M (low) to $89M (high), with a median of $78M. This range reflects the stage-progression result and conservative variance bounds around it. Key uncertainties include the absence of narrow-set peer multiples—reliance on a single method reduces confidence relative to a full triangulation. Investors should treat this range as indicative pending deeper operational and market diligence.
